mnemonics: OPEN INSIDE: A point is INTERIOR if you can fit an entire OPEN ball around it INSIDE the set.
keyPoints: An interior point of a set A has an open neighborhood completely contained in A.
The set of all interior points of A is called the interior of A, denoted by int(A).
The interior of any set is always an open set itself.
commonMistakes: Confusing an interior point with a limit point or a boundary point.
Assuming that a set is open if just one point has a neighborhood inside it, rather than every point.
